sentence_reviews.text: Distances, manifolds, transformations, invariants, and topology shape spatial and equivariant representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Bronstein et al. 2021 abstract lines 42-45 on geometric principles, regularities, and neural architectures; Gerken et al. 2021 abstract lines 38-40 on manifolds, equivariant maps, global symmetry, and representation theory; Bronstein et al. 2016 abstract lines 38-40 on Euclidean/grid structures, invariances, graphs, and manifolds.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources support geometry, manifolds, symmetries, invariance/equivariance, and representation-learning links. The exact combined list of distances, transformations, invariants, and topology is a synthesized atlas definition rather than a single directly stated claim.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Geometry, topology & symmetry is to connect Linear algebra, Calculus & automatic differentiation theory to downstream capabilities including Images, video & spatial grids, Sets, point clouds & meshes, Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 662-690 on computer vision, image formation, the 3D world, reconstruction, and geometry from a single view; AIMA lines 704-715 on configuration space, motion planning, Voronoi diagrams, trajectory optimization, and control; Bronstein et al. 2021 abstract lines 43-45 on feature learning, gradient descent/backpropagation, and unified geometric principles for CNNs/RNNs/GNNs/Transformers; Bronstein et al. 2016 abstract lines 38-40 on grid-like, invariant, graph, and manifold settings; Qi et al. 2016 abstract lines 38-39 on point clouds and permutation invariance; Li et al. 2021 abstract lines 38-40 on positional encoding for sequences/images and spatial multidimensional positions.
sentence_reviews.notes: The downstream areas are supported across inspected sources, and Bronstein et al. connect geometric principles with gradient-based deep learning. The exact graph role statement and dependency-to-downstream bridge are editorial synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Images, video & spatial grids
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 662-690 on computer vision, image formation, CNN image classification, the 3D world, reconstruction, and geometry from a single view; Bronstein et al. 2016 abstract lines 38-40 on Euclidean/grid-like structure and invariances in deep models; Bronstein et al. 2021 abstract lines 44-45 on unified geometric principles for CNNs and other architectures.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support geometry and invariance as relevant to images, computer vision, and grid-like data. 'Video' and the phrase 'substrate for' are not directly stated in the cited passages, so this is synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Sets, point clouds & meshes
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Bronstein et al. 2016 abstract lines 38-40 on non-Euclidean spaces, meshed surfaces, graphs, manifolds, and geometric deep learning; Qi et al. 2016 abstract lines 38-39 on point clouds as geometric data structures and permutation invariance of input points; Qi et al. 2017 abstract lines 38-40 on point sets in metric spaces, metric-space distances, and 3D point clouds.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources support point clouds, point sets, metric spaces, meshed surfaces, and geometric/non-Euclidean learning. The exact combined atlas category and 'substrate for' wording are synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Vaswani et al. 2017 abstract lines 42-44 on the Transformer dispensing with recurrence and convolutions; Li et al. 2021 abstract lines 38-40 on positional encoding for sequences/images, multidimensional positions, pixel positions, L2 distances, and positional relationships; Bronstein et al. 2021 abstract lines 43-45 on representation learning and geometric principles for Transformers and other architectures.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support positional and spatial coordinate encodings and their use in attention/Transformer-style architectures. The temporal part is only indirectly supported through sequence position, so this should not be marked verified.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Calculus & automatic differentiation theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 768-774 on mathematical background including vectors, matrices, and linear algebra; AIMA lines 736-741 on limits of AI and the mathematical objection; Bronstein et al. 2021 abstract lines 43-45 on regularities, gradient descent/backpropagation, and geometric principles; Li et al. 2021 abstract lines 38-40 on positional relationships and distances in a specific encoding setting.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support mathematical background, linear-algebra relevance, geometric assumptions/regularities, and gradient-based computation context. They do not directly state the empirical-mismatch warning or the direct-dependency list in this exact form.

sentence_reviews.text: Geometry, topology & symmetry rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 768-774 on vectors, matrices, and linear algebra as mathematical background; Gerken et al. 2021 abstract lines 38-40 on vector bundles, equivariant maps, intertwiners, Fourier analysis, Wigner matrices, spherical harmonics, Clebsch-Gordan coefficients, and representation theory; Qi et al. 2017 abstract lines 38-40 on metric-space distances and point-set features.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence supports linear algebraic objects and operations as central to geometric/equivariant neural-network formalisms. The exact 'rests on' dependency phrasing is an atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Geometry, topology & symmetry rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Calculus & automatic differentiation theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Bronstein et al. 2021 abstract lines 43-45 on representation learning, local gradient-descent methods, backpropagation, and geometric principles; Gerken et al. 2021 abstract lines 38-40 on arbitrary manifolds, sections of vector bundles, and equivariant maps; Baydin et al. 2015 abstract lines 3-6 and 23-30 on automatic differentiation and derivatives in machine learning.
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports calculus/AD as relevant to gradient-based learning and manifolds/geometric neural-network formalisms. It does not directly state the atlas dependency sentence verbatim.

edge_reviews.notes: AIMA identifies linear algebra as mathematical background, and the equivariant/geometric sources use vector bundles, equivariant maps, representation theory, metric spaces, and distance-based point-set learning. This supports a core prerequisite relation, although the edge is synthesized rather than explicitly named by one source.

Geometric deep learning is framed around gradient descent/backpropagation, manifolds, and differentiable/equivariant maps; Baydin et al. support AD as derivative computation for machine learning. This makes the required/core relationship defensible, but it remains a synthesis across sources.
